expectations.text: Identifies that database/sql I/O inside the domain Order type is a smell — the reason points outward at a concrete
Recommends a repository-style interface (port) owned by the domain/reason
Moves the SQL into an adapter/connection (e.g. internal/postgres), leaving the domain free of database/sql
expectations.evidence: internal/order/postgres/repository.go with compile-time var _ order.Repository check

expectations.text: Keeps the order-total business rule in the domain, not pushed into the adapter
expectations.evidence: Place() stays pure: validate, sum Total, set Status

expectations.text: Notes the construction/wiring belongs in cmd (how it's run)
expectations.evidence: main is the composition root: opens db, wires repo -> service
